2015-01-26.title: Two Japanese Hostages, as Different as Can Be, Linked by Fate in Syria
2015-01-26.abstract: The surviving hostage is a respected journalist, while the other man was a lost soul who had attempted suicide and seemed to become unhinged by setbacks in life.

2015-01-26.title: German Quandary of How to Deal With Anti-Immigration Movement
2015-01-26.abstract: Local leaders have started engaging with supporters of the movement, despite concerns about its roots and backing from neo-Nazis and extremists.

2015-01-26.title: W.H.O. Members Endorse Resolution to Improve Response to Health Emergencies
2015-01-26.abstract: Key provisions include creating a global cadre of emergency health workers, establishing a fund that could be tapped quickly, and support for the development of treatments for emerging infectious diseases.

2015-01-26.title: At Least 18 Die in Egyptian Violence
2015-01-26.abstract: The deaths came on the fourth anniversary of the Arab Spring uprising, a reminder of the ruthless crackdown the military-backed government has used to silence any echoes of that revolt.

2015-01-26.title: Disillusioned by War, Israeli Soldiers Muted in 1967 Are Given Fuller Voice
2015-01-26.abstract: As the International Criminal Court considers a war crimes investigation in the recent Gaza conflict, a new documentary film is showcasing previously unaired accounts of the brutal experiences of an earlier generation of Israeli soldiers.

2015-01-27.title: Egyptian Is Convicted of Genital Mutilation
2015-01-27.abstract: Officials said it was Egypt’s first criminal conviction for the practice of female genital mutilation, and related to the death of a 13-year-old girl.

2015-01-27.title: Killing of Protester Prompts Rare Criticism From Egyptian State Newspaper
2015-01-27.abstract: The death of the protester, whose body was riddled with shotgun pellets, was condemned in an unusually frank editorial on the front page of Al Ahram.

2015-01-27.title: U.S. and India Share Sense of Unease Over China
2015-01-27.abstract: President Obama found that his concerns were closely aligned with those of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and the two leaders agreed to a joint statement chiding Beijing.

2015-01-27.title: Israel: Dissident Reservists Dismissed
2015-01-27.abstract: The military on Monday terminated the service of 43 reservists who in September signed a letter refusing to participate in “actions against Palestinians” for moral reasons.

2015-01-27.title: Argentine Leader Attacks Spy Agency Amid Furor Over Prosecutor’s Death
2015-01-27.abstract: Argentina’s president, Cristina Fernández de Kirchner, accused rogue factions of trying to sabotage a deal with Iran to investigate a 1994 bombing that the prosecutor, Alberto Nisman, was looking into.

2015-01-28.title: Life of Italian Nobility for Sale, Complete With Regulations and Taxes
2015-01-28.abstract: Italy’s long heritage of inherited wealth and property has withered thanks to cuts in government subsidies and high maintenance costs, among other factors.

2015-01-28.title: Ukraine: E.U. Sends Aid to East
2015-01-28.abstract: The European Union delivered three planeloads of aid to eastern Ukraine on Tuesday, including blankets, tents, heaters and warm clothing.

2015-01-28.title: Mexico Officially Declares Missing Students Dead
2015-01-28.abstract: Four months after the students were abducted, Mexico’s attorney general said confessions and forensic evidence supported the theory that their bodies were incinerated near a garbage dump.

2015-01-28.title: Russian Movie ‘Leviathan’ Gets Applause in Hollywood but Scorn at Home
2015-01-28.abstract: The film has admirers and won a Golden Globe, but some Russian detractors want it banned and accuse its director of acting under Western orders.

2015-01-28.title: Egypt Condemns Western Outrage at Fatal Shooting of Protester
2015-01-28.abstract: The Foreign Ministry expressed dismay that the killing of a female activist in Cairo had drawn widespread condemnation from the West.

2015-01-29.title: Michelle Obama Praised for Bold Stand She Didn’t Take in Saudi Arabia
2015-01-29.abstract: The first lady observed longstanding diplomatic protocol by not covering her hair during a visit with the new Saudi leader this week.

2015-01-29.title: In Pakistan, a Charity Project Points to Official Tolerance of Militants
2015-01-29.abstract: On Monday, Hafiz Muhammad Saeed, the founder of Lashkar-e-Taiba, the group that waged the deadly 2008 Mumbai attacks, inaugurated an ambulance service run by the group’s charity wing, Jamaat-ud-Dawa, in Karachi.

2015-01-29.title: Ukraine Clamps Down on Travel to and From Rebel Areas
2015-01-29.abstract: After new border restrictions were put in place, a policy of isolating and frustrating rebel-held regions has taken a major and maddening turn.

2015-01-29.title: Iran Bows to Pressure in Naming U.N. Envoy
2015-01-29.abstract: An announcement of the appointment came nine months after the United States had made clear that Iran’s first choice, Hamid Aboutalebi, would never get a visa.

2015-01-29.title: Canada Agency Monitors File-Sharing, Reports Say
2015-01-29.abstract: A document leaked by Edward J. Snowden outlines an effort by the Canadian electronic spying agency to detect possible extremists; some privacy experts expressed concerns.

2015-01-30.title: Italy Opens Voting for New President. Let the Intrigue Begin.
2015-01-30.abstract: In a process expected to take at least three days, the candidate of Prime Minister Matteo Renzi has already been turned away.

2015-01-30.title: Group of Senators Seeks to Lift Cuba Travel Limits
2015-01-30.abstract: A bipartisan group of lawmakers has taken the first step in a looming congressional battle over how far to expand trade and tourism with the Castro government.

2015-01-30.title: Senators Introduce Cuba Travel Bill
2015-01-30.abstract: A bipartisan group of senators spoke in support of a bill that would remove a ban on Americans traveling to Cuba, in light of President Obama’s restoration of diplomatic relations.

2015-01-30.title: Plane Crashes Near Aruba After Pursuit by Military Jets
2015-01-30.abstract: A small civilian aircraft, suspected of carrying drugs and pursued by Venezuelan military jets, crashed Thursday into the Caribbean Sea near the island of Aruba, officials said.

2015-01-30.title: As Venezuelan Oil Prices Fall, Social Programs Suffer
2015-01-30.abstract: Venezuelans have put up with shortages and long lines for years, but the economic situation has recently grown dire.

2015-01-31.title: U.N. Rights Chief Says He’ll Shine a Light on Countries Big and Small
2015-01-31.abstract: Four months into his new job, Prince Zeid Ra’ad Zeid al-Hussein of Jordan is resisting the efforts of some nations to hold his office to a narrow interpretation of its mandate.

2015-01-31.title: Odd Photo Choice for Venezuela Promotional Effort
2015-01-31.abstract: Jim Wyss, a reporter for The Miami Herald, was surprised to see a picture of himself after his release from Venezuelan detention being used in a campaign for national pride.

2015-01-31.title: Venezuela: Military Says It Downed Plane Near Aruba
2015-01-31.abstract: Venezuela’s defense minister said Friday that Venezuelan military aircraft had shot down a small plane that crashed a day earlier in the waters near the island of Aruba.

2015-01-31.title: Canada Seeks to Strengthen Spy Agency After Attacks
2015-01-31.abstract: Prime Minister Stephen Harper introduced legislation that would greatly expand the role of the spy service, allowing courts to remove online postings and increasing police detention powers.

2015-01-31.title: A Strained Alliance: Obama-Netanyahu Rift Grew Over Years
2015-01-31.abstract: The diplomatic break touched off by Benjamin Netanyahu’s decision to negotiate an address to Congress without first telling President Obama reflects their fundamentally different world views.
